q: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
exp: Au comes from the Latin 'aurum'. Silver is Ag (argentum).

q: What is the approximate speed of light in a vacuum?
exp: Light travels about 300,000 km per second — 7 times around Earth in one second.

q: Who proposed the theory of relativity?
exp: Einstein published special relativity in 1905 and general relativity in 1915.

q: What is the most abundant chemical element in the Universe?
exp: Hydrogen makes up about 75% of the Universe's ordinary matter.

q: What is the number of protons in an atom called?
exp: The atomic number defines the element — hydrogen is 1, carbon is 6.

q: Which force is responsible for attraction between electric charges?
exp: It is one of the four fundamental forces of nature.

q: Which scientist formulated the laws of motion and universal gravitation?
What is the pH of a neutral solution?
exp: Below 7 is acidic; above 7 is basic (alkaline).
Human DNA has about 3 billion base pairs.

q: What is the SI unit of force?
exp: 1 newton gives a 1 kg mass an acceleration of 1 m/s².

q: Which subatomic particle has no electric charge?
exp: The neutron is neutral; it sits in the nucleus with the protons.

q: What is the theory explaining the origin of the Universe?
exp: The Big Bang says the Universe expanded from a point ~13.8 billion years ago.
